W1_selective_prediction.method: Sort 75 cases by maxprob confidence descending, take top 37, mean mIoU

W2_vulnerability_scoping.original_claim: Sophisticated models MORE fragile: PN2 gap=0.317 > DGCNN gap=0.250 > PN gap=0.188
W2_vulnerability_scoping.scoped_claim: Among reliably converging deep learning architectures, model complexity correlates with vulnerability gap (PN2: 0.317 > DGCNN: 0.250 > PointNet: 0.188). RF's larger gap (0.360) reflects hand-crafted feature limitations rather than model complexity, while PT's near-zero gap reflects baseline-level performance under both protocols.

RF.note: Simplest; large gap from feature-space limitations

W3_english_labels.note: Use English labels in all tables/figures for JDR. Add parenthetical Chinese for reproducibility.

title_note: Consider 'Performance Degradation' instead of 'Performance Collapse' — DGCNN drops 28%, PN 20% (degradation), while PN2 drops 36%, RF 40% (collapse-level). Alternative: use both terms in abstract to scope model-dependent severity.
jdr_format_note: JDR Short Communication: ≤2500 words, 2-3 display items. Proposed: Table 1 (dual-protocol) + Table 2 (clinical metrics) + Figure 1 (learning curve). Table 3 (per-type) → supplementary.
